summary: Iteration-4 testing (DMs + mentions + reactions + desktop notifications UI). Backend: 17/17 new iter4 tests + 59/59 regression (iter-1/2/3) = 76/76 PASSING. Frontend: DM flow PASS (new-dm-button, new-dm-modal, new-dm-search, new-dm-user-<email>, dm-item-<email>, send message, sidebar shows other_user_name). Mentions PASS (composer-textarea: typing `@` opens mention-popover with mention-option-<email>; selecting inserts `@Name ` and the mentions list is recorded). Reactions PASS (open-reaction-picker-<id> button visible on hover, picker shows 8 emoji, clicking pick-reaction-<emoji>-<id> renders reaction-<emoji>-<id> chip, clicking chip again toggles it off). Desktop notifications UI: toggle-notifications-button NOT RENDERED on /profile — `NotificationsSection` is defined in ProfilePage.jsx (line 74) but is never included in the page's main render block (line 41-51 only mounts AccountInfo, PasswordChange, PasswordResetRequest). This is a real frontend bug.

ui_bugs.issue: Desktop notifications section is not mounted. NotificationsSection() is defined but never referenced in ProfilePage's <main>; toggle-notifications-button is therefore absent from the DOM.

issues: When sending '...DM@ hello' the literal `@` is rendered inline against the previous word with no space (cosmetic; matches input). Mention popover correctly does NOT trigger when @ is preceded by a non-space, per regex `(?:^|\s)@`. No fix required.
Pre-existing from prior iterations: Emergent preview badge (bottom-right) overlaps the Send button hitbox. Carryover.

action_items: Fix /app/frontend/src/pages/ProfilePage.jsx — mount <NotificationsSection /> inside the <main> block (e.g., between AccountInfo and PasswordChange, or after PasswordResetRequest). The component is fully implemented (lines 74-150) but never rendered.
critical_code_review_comments: ProfilePage: dead code path — NotificationsSection is implemented but unmounted. Either render it or delete it. (Action item above.)

routes/dms.py uses channel name `dm:<a>:<b>` as the uniqueness key but does NOT add a unique index on (name,type). Concurrent POST /api/dms by both participants could race and create duplicate DM docs. Consider a unique index on `channels.name` (already present from iter-1?) — verify it covers DM names.

routes/messages.py react endpoint reuses _assert_channel_access which already enforces archived/private membership — good. Reaction broadcast payload includes only `reactions` map (no full message), which is intentional and lighter; FE must already merge by message_id (ChatPage subscribes to message:reactions per spec).
models.MessagePublic.reactions is typed `dict` (untyped value). Consider `Dict[str, List[str]]` for OpenAPI clarity.
DM unread count uses string compare on ISO timestamps (same pattern as iter-3 unread). Consistent across the codebase, so safe — but flagged again as a future migration target to native datetime.
GET /api/dms issues N+1 queries (last message + unread per DM). Acceptable for now; aggregate pipeline would be cleaner at scale.
Self-mention filter in messages.post_message uses set comprehension which also de-duplicates mentions — good.
AdminPage.jsx still ~1.2k lines (carryover from iter-3); ProfilePage now ~350 lines — both could be split.

success_rate.backend: 100% (76/76: 17 new iter4 + 50 iter-1/2 regression + 9 iter-3 unread)
success_rate.frontend: ~85% (DM flow ✓, mention popover ✓, mention insert ✓, reactions add+toggle ✓, sidebar DM section + preview ✓; toggle-notifications-button ✗ — not rendered)

test_credentials: Admin: operations@panoramacoaches.com.au / Pano3666 (unchanged). Admin display name restored to 'Operations Admin', avatar_url cleared.
seed_data_creation: During backend tests created (and then auto-cleaned via fixtures): users with prefixes iter4a/iter4b/iter4c/iter4dead, ephemeral channels (mentest-*, selfmen-*, reacttest-*, reactpriv-*, test-pub-*, test-priv-*, brand_user_*, reset_*). For frontend testing created user fe_iter4_884e0d@example.com — DELETED at end of run. DM channels with that user remain orphaned in the DB (no API to list/delete DMs of deleted users); harmless but worth noting.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter-4 backend is rock-solid. The single open frontend bug is purely a JSX wiring issue in /app/frontend/src/pages/ProfilePage.jsx — render the existing NotificationsSection component inside <main>. After that, re-run frontend test from this iteration's script (DM + mention + reactions still PASS today). For mention-highlight/mentions-me-badge verification you need the mentioned user's session to view the message — I did not log into bob in the browser (would clobber admin cookies); a Playwright incognito context or a separate request-based check is the cleanest path.
rca of the issue: ProfilePage.jsx defines NotificationsSection() at line 74 with the toggle-notifications-button data-testid (line 114) and the proper Notification.permission state machine, but the parent component's JSX (lines 41-51) does not include <NotificationsSection />. Reproduction: open /profile as any user → expected 'Desktop notifications' card with 'Enable'/'Unsupported' button → actual: only Identity, Security, Recovery cards. Mitigation (2-line fix): inside <main className="...space-y-8 md:space-y-10"> add `<NotificationsSection />`.
